evals.prompt: I have a photo of our Event Storming board from a workshop we did for Joe's Pizza, a local pizza delivery shop. Please interpret this board and produce an ESML file. The board image is at inputs/joes-pizza-event-storming-board.png. I also have two session transcripts: inputs/DDD Meets GenAI - 2025-09-06 - EventStorming Joe's Pizza.txt and inputs/DDD Meets GenAI - 2025-09-08 - Tactical EventStorming Joe's Pizza.txt
evals.expected_output: A valid ESML YAML file capturing all stickies visible on the Joe's Pizza board — events, commands, aggregates, actors, policies, read models, external systems, hotspots — with correct origin provenance, sequence numbers, group assignments, and cross-references. Should detect this as a design_level board.
files: /sessions/keen-kind-albattani/mnt/xp-2026/inputs/DDD Meets GenAI - 2025-09-08 - Tactical EventStorming Joe's Pizza.txt

evals.prompt: Here's a photo of our Event Storming board. It's a Big Picture session — we only got as far as identifying the main events and some actors. Please interpret it into ESML format. Board image: inputs/joes-pizza-event-storming-board.png (Note: for this test, treat it as if it were a big-picture board — only extract events and actors, ignore commands/aggregates.)
evals.expected_output: A valid ESML YAML file with board.variation set to big_picture, containing only events, actors, hotspots, and groups. Should NOT include commands, aggregates, policies, or read models. Should still have correct provenance and sequence numbers.

evals.prompt: I have the Joe's Pizza Event Storming board at inputs/joes-pizza-event-storming-board.png. Interpret just the board image into ESML — no transcripts available this time. I'd like the ESML output so I can pass it to our domain modeler later.
evals.expected_output: A valid ESML YAML file for the full design-level board, produced without transcript assistance. Some element names might be less polished (relying purely on visual OCR), but the structure, relationships, and provenance should be complete. Demonstrates the skill works without transcripts.
